title: State aims at 32% growth in mining sector, says Minister Kollu Ravindra
content: Government accelerating exploration for gold, beach sand minerals, iron ore, and manganese, he says, adding that gold deposits have been identified in Anantapur and Chittoor districts

title: Assault on human dignity, says Rahul Gandhi on incident of bonded labour in U.P.
content: Uttar Pradesh Congress leader Ajay Kumar Lallu said the failed economic policies of the BJP government were responsible for such oppression of the ordinary poor people

title: Fake forensic report conspiracy: BJP Haryana chief corners Punjab CM Mann, demands immediate resignation
content: Ms. Gupta alleged that after the video surfaced, the Punjab government and police “exerted all efforts” to prove it fake

title: Assam couple booked for another birthday celebration on Brahmaputra bridge
content: Electronic sparklers and firecrackers were used to celebrate the birthday of Surajit Mandal’s wife by allegedly blocking a portion of the bridge

title: Muharram procession passes off peacefully in Srinagar amid police warning over hoisting flags, pics of terrorists
content: Shia mourners, including women and children, assembled in Srinagar’s Guru Bazar around 6 a.m. and the procession culminated at Dalgate around 9 a.m.

title: BJP imposing vegetarianism in Bengal, says Trinamool
content: It’s an attempt to deprive children of nutrition by denying them eggs with midday meal, TMC Rajya Sabha MP Derek O’ Brien says

title: 31% of Rajya Sabha MPs have declared criminal cases: ADR
content: ADR and National Election Watch analysis finds 16% of sitting Rajya Sabha MPs have declared serious criminal cases; 14% are billionaires

title: Air India plane strays into Pakistan airspace; DGCA takes action against air traffic controller, crew
content: DGCA said that after the aircraft commenced approach during radar vectoring, it briefly entered the Pakistan airspace

title: J&K govt. bids for Sufiyana music’s inclusion in UNESCO’s intangible heritage list
content: J&K Chief Minister Omar Abdullah has written a formal letter to Union Minister of Culture Gajendra Singh Shekhawat and pressed for its inclusion in the UNESCO list

title: Monsoon tracker updates on June 24, 2026: Assam government monitoring flash flood situation in Arunachal
content: In view of the heavy rainfall in the upper catchments, a substantial rise in water levels and flow velocity is anticipated in the Brahmaputra river and its tributaries in the downstream areas of Assam

title: Kolkata under-construction warehouse collapse updates: Five killed, several injured, in Taratala accident; rescue efforts on
content: According to locals present at the accident site, 50 to 60 workers were working at the site when the accident took place

title: Bihar Cabinet gives nod to Bihar State Teacher Transfer, Rules 2026 for transfer of school teachers across the State
content: It also approves name of Justice (rtd) Vinod Kumar Sinha of Patna high court to head Inquiry Commission into Bharat Bhushan Tiwari encounter.

title: Mehbooba Mufti attends Pandit ceremony to mend broken bonds between communities in Kashmir
content: Ms. Mufti’s move to attend the ceremony has come at a time when many displaced Pandits groups are mulling to return to Kashmir in the wake of the improvement in security scenario and a dip in militant recruitment

title: Anna Hazare warns of mass agitation unless Maharashtra withdraws ‘RTI amendments’
content: Hazare said that if the government does not scrap the amendments before July 5, he would start a nation-wide agitation

title: PACOM, the deeper meaning behind a dropped prefix
content: The U.S.’s reversion from INDOPACOM to PACOM suggests that its regional shifts run deeper than a simple name change

title: Suspended officer retracts allegations against senior official over Lucknow fire
content: The suspended Fire Station Second Officer also issued a public apology for an earlier video in which he had accused the Lucknow chief fire officer of lapses linked to the incident

title: Thrikkakara municipality installing fencing along road after fatal fall of girl triggers protests
content: Thrikkakara municipality decides to erect fence using its own funds. State Human Rights Commission orders a high-level probe into the incident

title: Yogi says Uttar Pradesh received investment proposals worth ₹50,000 crore
content: Uttar Pradesh has climbed out of ‘Bimaru’ (ailing) status to a revenue surplus state and an attractive investment destination now, Yogi Adityanath claims

title: VCK wants CM Vijay to reconsider gold ring scheme
content: Vijay’s remarks in the Assembly, in which he appeared to mock the electoral loss of former CM Stalin in Kolathur, were not “befitting the dignity associated with the office of the Chief Minister”, says Thirumavalavan

title: Two persons arrested for stealing copper coils from borewells near Jolarpet
content: Surveillance was increased in farming villages in the region that had more than 350 motor pump sets and borewells to irrigate paddy fields

title: Symbolism or substance? T.N.’s public health professionals question gold ring scheme
content: The gold ring may be intended as a celebration of birth, but for many within the public health sector, the more pressing need is investment in the workforce and infrastructure that makes safe deliveries possible, say health officials and doctors

title: CMC bags Centre of Excellence award for diabetes
content: The CMC holds the distinction of being both an International Diabetes Federation Centre of Excellence in Diabetes Care and an Indian Council of Medical Research Collaborating Centre of Excellence for Diabetes

title: Woman rescued after factory catches fire near Gudiyatham
content: The police said that it was around 11 a.m when a few workers noticed thick smoke coming from the unit

title: PM Modi receives call from Armenia PM, thanks him for helping evacuation of Indians from Iran
content: Mr. Modi also congratulated Mr. Pashinyan on the victory of his Civil Contract Party in the country's parliamentary elections and said India looks forward to working closely together to take India-Armenia relations to new heights Mr.

title: Video showing Higher Education Minister touching girl athletes’ legs sparks outrage
content: Video captures him touching outstretched legs of athletes and cracking their toes while another guest was seen shaking hands with them; a complaint has been lodged at the DGP’s office against the Minister by lawyer Saranya Natarajan of the DMK’s Legal wing.

title: Kolkata under-construction warehouse collapse: Amid death and despair, kin of workers search for the living
content: There is no clarity as to how many workers were working at the site when accident occurred on June 24 afternoon; Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari  said that most of the workers were brought to work at the site from Bihar

title: India, UAE review strategic defence partnership amid talks over BrahMos missile sale
content: The UAE is assessing options to strengthen its air and missile defence capabilities following attacks on critical installations during the recent conflict in West Asia

title: Flash floods hit six districts in Arunachal; one dead, four missing
content: Assam government issues high alert in districts downstream of the affected areas in Arunachal Pradesh, while overnight rainfall cuts off West Sikkim district headquarters

title: Amid political turbulence, Diya Binu keeps her focus on governance
content: The Pala municipal chairperson is steadily growing into a role that demands far more than her age might suggest

title: Fiancee or lover: Who pushed Ketan Agarwal off the cliff
content: Police are examining forensic evidence to determine whether the fatal shove was administered by Siya Goyal or her lover; Mr. Chaudhary’s decision to wear a hoodie on a hot day roused police suspicions

title: Rahul Gandhi expresses regret in defamation case filed by Shivraj Singh Chouhan's son
content: Kartikeya Singh has filed a defamation complaint in a Bhopal court against the Leader of Opposition in the Lok Sabha. Gandhi's application, filed by his lawyer, said that his statement was not related to Singh.

title: VHP seeks FIR, fast track court to look into Ram Temple donation allegations
content: “FIR should be filed at the first place. Then investigation needs to be expedited and fast rack court should take-up the matter on day to Day basis,” he said, adding that guilty persons must get punishment

title: China stands ready to work with India, says Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson
content: Beijing is ready to work with New Delhi to enhance mutual trust and dispel doubts, says Lin Jian, the spokesperson; This comes after Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi met PM Modi on June 23, 2026

title: Shiv Sena (UBT) MPs meet Speaker, demand letter submitted by leaders joining Shinde camp
content: Shiv Sena (UBT) has questioned the lack of transparency surrounding the entry of six MPs into the ruling Shiv Sena led by Eknath Shinde; it had submitted a letter to the Speaker seeking a hearing on this.

title: Farmer's daughter Anushka Yadav scripts history, becomes India's youngest ...
content: Eighteen-year-old Anushka Yadav etched her name in history at the National Inter-State Championships, becoming the youngest Indian athletics national record holder. She shattered the women's hammer throw record with a remarkable 67.02m, surpassing the previous mark twice during the competition. Hailing from a farming family, Yadav's journey from rural Uttar Pradesh to national glory is an inspiring tale of dedication and talent.

title: Michael Dell beats Oracle's Larry Ellison to make place in world’s 5 richest people
content: Michael Dell has surged into the world's top 5 richest individuals, surpassing Larry Ellison. This ascent is fueled by Dell Technologies' booming AI infrastructure business, which saw a remarkable 757% surge in AI server revenue.  While Dell's wealth has climbed significantly, Ellison's fortune has seen a dip, leading to this notable shift in global wealth rankings.

title: After laying off hundreds of engineers, GM swaps 1,000 workers with 50 robots
content: General Motors has introduced 50 collaborative robots at its Michigan Factory Zero, reportedly leading to over 1,000 job eliminations. This move, aimed at cost-cutting and efficiency, sparks criticism from the United Auto Workers union, which views it as a threat to jobs. GM claims the robots support human workers, but the UAW president calls it a "fight for humanity" amid broader automation concerns in the industry.

title: When data met Donald – and died: How US Prez trumped India with fake tariff numbers
content: A new book reveals President Trump's disregard for factual trade data concerning India, often dismissing official figures as 'bullshit numbers' to justify his imposition of high tariffs.  Despite evidence from USTR and trade representatives, Trump insisted on inflated tariff rates, leading to strained US-India relations.  This approach extended to foreign policy, where his administration prioritized 'feelings over facts,' impacting global partnerships.

title: Japan’s second richest man SoftBank’s Masayoshi Son junks his plan to retire in sixties
content: SoftBank's Masayoshi Son, 68, plans to lead the tech giant for another decade, focusing intensely on artificial intelligence and robotics.  Dismissing 'AI bubble' fears as 'blasphemy,' Son believes AI's potential is just unfolding.  He aims to achieve 'artificial superintelligence' and is backing this vision with significant investments in companies like Arm and OpenAI, alongside robotics acquisitions.

title: Karnataka tourist slips from rocks, drowns off Goa coast
content: A tourist from Karnataka tragically drowned in North Goa after slipping from treacherous rocks near Baga-Arpora. Ashpaq Masli, 34, ignored monsoon warnings and ventured onto the slippery terrain for photos, a common but dangerous practice.  Witnesses saw him lose his footing and get swept away by the sea.  Authorities continue to urge caution despite installed signboards highlighting the risks.

title: Dustin Poirier opens up on airport arrest and life after UFC retirement
content: Dustin Poirier has admitted he needs help after video of his Atlanta airport arrest was released by the Atlanta Police Department. The retired UFC star said adjusting to life after fighting has been difficult and acknowledged that alcohol is not helping. In an emotional Instagram message, Poirier said he wants to protect his family and take the right steps to improve his life moving forward.

title: Josh Hokit stands by Michelle Obama remark despite White, Cormier backlash
content: Josh Hokit has defended his controversial Michelle Obama remark, saying he believed it was a compliment and an example of free speech. His explanation has not reduced criticism, with figures such as Dana White and Daniel Cormier strongly condemning the comment. Hokit, however, continues to stand by his words and has refused to apologize.

title: Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo face off in football’s net worth race
content: Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi have spent nearly two decades battling for football supremacy. This time, the contest is about money. Both legends have crossed the billionaire mark and stand among the richest athletes in the world. Their fortunes have surged through football contracts, sponsorships, business ventures, and social media influence. While Ronaldo is believed to hold a slight edge in total wealth, Messi is not far behind in this remarkable financial race.

title: Deepti Sharma on verge of history as India star eyes world record vs Bangladesh
content: Indian all-rounder Deepti Sharma is on the cusp of cricket history, needing just one wicket against Bangladesh to become the leading wicket-taker in women's international cricket, surpassing Jhulan Goswami.  Despite a recent loss, Deepti's consistent performance, especially her five-wicket haul, highlights her prowess.  However, Team India faces middle-order batting concerns and fielding lapses ahead of this crucial World Cup encounter.

title: Punjab farmers protest India-US trade deal, burn effigies of PM Modi, Trump
content: Farmers in Punjab protested the proposed India-US trade agreement, fearing it will harm domestic agriculture, labourers, and small traders. Burning effigies of leaders, they demanded the pact's cancellation, citing concerns over increased imports and unfair competition from subsidized American farmers. This opposition echoes past farm law debates, with calls for MSP guarantees and irrigation reforms.

title: Horoscope today: Daily astrological predictions for June 25, 2026
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for June 25, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: Packers, Cowboys emerge as leading trade destinations for Steelers star
content: The Pittsburgh Steelers’ star player Alex Highsmith has returned to voluntary workouts but he has been linked to a number of teams ahead of the new season as he focuses on training. Amid all the mess, the Dallas Cowboys and the Green Bay Packers have emerged as potential landing spots for the star player.

title: French mother, 5 children rescued from Pak home after decade-long captivity
content: A French mother and her five children have been dramatically rescued from a decade of alleged captivity and abuse in Pakistan's Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province. Sylvie Yasmina, who claims her husband 'effectively imprisoned' them after moving from Australia in 2014, was found with visible injuries. Her son's brave escape to a police station led to the family's liberation and her expressed desire to return to France.

title: 'Will leave only after killing you': How a water tap dispute turned fatal for Bengaluru PG owner
content: A paying guest owner in Bengaluru tragically died after being brutally assaulted by two students over a dispute involving a water tap. The youths returned hours after an initial confrontation, issuing death threats. The owner, attempting to defend his family, was overpowered and fatally attacked with a cricket bat. His son recounted his father's dying wish to file a complaint and ensure justice.

title: Watch: BMC staffer falls into pothole during Mumbai mayor's flood inspection
content: During Mumbai Mayor Ritu Tawde's inspection of waterlogged Gandhi Market, a civic staffer fell into a channel but was quickly rescued. The incident sparked political criticism, with an MP accusing the mayor of inaction and corruption. Despite the mishap, officials stated the situation was managed. The area, prone to flooding, is now equipped with a recently commissioned ₹19 crore underground pumping station designed to handle heavy rainfall.

title: India's 2036 Olympic bid gets timeline: IOC reveals when the host will be finalised
content: India's 2036 Olympic hosting aspirations gained momentum as the IOC approved a new selection process, aiming for a host announcement by mid-2029.  This revamped system introduces a "Strategic Dialogue" phase for greater transparency and clarity.  Candidates, including India with Ahmedabad as the proposed city, will undergo rigorous evaluation, including financial guarantees and site visits, before a final decision is made.

title: Chhattisgarh grocer confesses to killing 8 men in 4 months, tested poison on stray first
content: A grocery store owner in Chhattisgarh has confessed to a chilling serial poisoning spree that claimed eight lives over four months. Ramsahay Jaiswal allegedly used borax powder mixed in liquor to eliminate acquaintances over petty grievances, including loan burdens, perceived black magic, political arguments, and land disputes. He even feigned concern for his victims. The killings were uncovered after a pattern of deaths emerged, leading to his arrest and confession.

title: ‘I had one week to live’: Muni Long reveals double lung transplant following Lupus
content: Muni Long, a Grammy-winning artist, bravely divulged her challenging health ordeal that culminated in a double lung transplant amidst a tour-related crisis. Faced with a grim prognosis of just one week to live, she confronted a profound dilemma: hospice care or surgery. Her wish to live for her son guided her choice, and the transplant surprisingly improved her singing voice, although she remains hesitant about performing.

title: Another top US army officer abruptly steps down under war secy Hegseth's watch
content: General Christopher Donahue, the last American soldier to exit Afghanistan in 2021, is stepping down as commander of US Army Europe and Africa after just 18 months. His departure aligns with Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth's initiative to reduce senior military leadership. The Army is reportedly considering downgrading the command, a move that follows criticism of European allies.

title: Hot country, cold shoulder: Pew survey shows Trump's global approval tanking
content: Global opinion on Donald Trump is overwhelmingly negative, with a median of just 23% expressing confidence in his leadership. A new Pew survey reveals widespread disapproval of his handling of foreign policy, including Iran and trade tariffs. Confidence in the US as a reliable partner has plummeted, particularly in Canada and Europe, painting a stark contrast to Trump's claims of global admiration.

title: GTA 6 Ultimate Edition pre-orders: Price, bonuses, release details and more
content: Pre-orders for Grand Theft Auto VI begin June 25, with the game launching November 19, 2026, on PS5 and Xbox Series X/S. Two editions, Standard ($79.99) and digital-only Ultimate ($99.99), are available. All pre-orders receive the Vintage Vice City Pack. No PC version is confirmed for launch, and physical copies will include a download code.

title: Speaker Birla to decide on TMC, Sena (UBT) defections before Monsoon Session
content: According to sources, legal and constitutional experts attached to Parliament are currently examining the matter and are expected to provide their recommendations before a final decision is taken. Previous rulings by presiding officers in comparable situations are also being studied to ensure any decision is legally and constitutionally sound. Apart from the rebel factions of the TMC and Shiv Sena (UBT), the DMK has also sought a separate seating arrangement following the collapse of its long-standing alliance with the Congress.

title: Sooryavanshi to get a separate changing room in England: ECB reveals why
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, recently called up to India’s national team, could become the country’s youngest international cricketer if he debuts against Ireland. During the England tour, he will use a separate changing room under ICC and ECB safeguarding rules for under-16 players. The ECB and BCCI are coordinating arrangements, while his parents are expected to accompany him throughout the tour.

title: Bill Gates says Jeffrey Epstein sought to blackmail him over extramarital affairs
content: Bill Gates revealed to US lawmakers that Jeffrey Epstein appeared to be contemplating blackmail over his extramarital affairs, citing "veiled" threats and draft emails. Gates, testifying before the House Oversight Committee, stated he was never blackmailed but acknowledged Epstein explored using personal information to maintain influence. He expressed regret over his association with the late sex offender, calling it a "grave error in judgment."

title: Watch: After 'successful curse' on Kane, Ghana witch doctor frees striker of spell
content: England's goalless World Cup draw with Ghana drew attention after Ghanaian witch doctor Nana Kwaku Bonsam claimed he had cursed Harry Kane and later lifted the hex following the striker's missed chance. The bizarre story sparked headlines, while pundits criticised England's flat performance and said they were fortunate to avoid defeat. Despite concerns, most believe Thomas Tuchel's side remains well placed ahead of its final group-stage match against Panama.

title: Can employees who never got appointment letters demand one now? What new labour code says
content: Employers must now issue appointment letters in a standardized format to all staff, including those in informal sectors, detailing job roles, pay, and benefits before work commences. This new mandate aims to prevent disputes and enhance clarity on service conditions. Existing employees also have three months to receive these letters. Non-compliance incurs significant financial penalties, with states expected to align their rules soon.

title: Trump Seeks $88 Billion In Extra Funding, Mostly For Iran War
content: US President Donald Trump asked Congress on Wednesday for nearly $88 billion in extra spending, most of it to cover the cost of the Iran war, at a moment of growing unease in both parties over the conflict and its political price.

title: Trump Clashes With Republicans Over Iran War
content: Four Republicans joined Democrats in adopting a largely symbolic resolution directing Trump to end hostilities with Iran or seek congressional authorization to continue them.

title: Israeli Strikes Kill 2 In Lebanon, Hezbollah Accuses It Of Truce Violation
content: Hezbollah drew Lebanon into the Middle East war on March 2 with rocket fire at Israel to avenge the killing of Iran's supreme leader in US-Israeli strikes.

title: "We Were Let Down" By Allies On Iran War: Trump Tells NATO Chief
content: US President Donald Trump told NATO chief Mark Rutte on Wednesday he was "let down" by members of the transatlantic alliance who did not back his war against Iran.

title: After Lucknow Tragedy, Coaching Centres, Libraries Sealed Across Districts
content: Lucknow Fire: In Lucknow, teams of the Lucknow Development Authority (LDA), the fire department and the electricity department continued inspections of educational institutions and commercial establishments.

title: Zohran Mamdani-Backed Leftist Candidates Win New York Democratic Primaries
content: Three candidates backed by New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani won Democratic primary elections for Congress on Tuesday, underlining the surge of the left in US politics as Democrats grow disillusioned with the party establishment.

title: Hantavirus Outbreak Should Formally End On July 2: WHO
content: The deadly hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship, which sparked international alarm, should be formally declared over on July 2, the World Health Organization (WHO) announced Wednesday.

title: India, EU Discuss Achievements, Challenges In Advancing Human Rights
content: India, EU described the discussions as meaningful, free and frank, highlighting the value of maintaining regular engagement on human rights issues.

title: Heavy Rainfall Triggers Landslides, Blocks Geyzing-Legship Road In Sikkim
content: According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), Geyzing recorded 95.2 mm of rainfall during the past 24 hours. District Magistrate of Geyzing, Tenzing Denzongpa, said information about the landslide was received at around 9 pm on Tuesday.

title: Mossad Planned Attack On Asim Munir In Switzerland, Analyst Claims
content: Brazilian journalist and geopolitical analyst Pepe Escobar made the claim during a conversation with Lebanese-Australian entrepreneur and political commentator Mario Nawfal on his X handle.

title: IndiGo, Air India's Flights Come Face-To-Face On Taxiway
content: Sources said the Air India aircraft registered VT TQV an A320 Neo with 164 passengers on board, was landing and the IndiGo flight was taking off when they came face to face at 7.59 pm.

title: Delhi's Central Secretariat To Become Triple-Interchange Metro Station
content: The new route is part of the Janakpuri West-RK Ashram Marg extension of the Magenta Line and will run entirely underground.

title: 'Could've Called Off Wedding, Why Kill Him?' Pune Fort Murder Victim's Father
content: Ketan Agarwal fell to his death in a gorge at the Lohagad Fort while trekking with Siya. Initially, it was declared an accidental death. Police probe, however, revealed a sinister plan.

title: Surat Man's Last Call Was To His Wife. He Died Hours Later In Qatar Blast
content: For more than 48 hours, his family waited desperately for any news regarding his whereabouts. The wait ended when his brother, Ravi Patel, who is also in Qatar, identified Sunny's body, shattering the family's final hopes.

title: J&K Caps Bus, Cab, Auto Fares For Amarnath Yatra
content: As per the order, fares have been fixed for key routes including Srinagar to Baltal, Baltal to Jammu, Srinagar to Pahalgam, Pahalgam to Jammu, and Srinagar to Jammu.

title: Rs 17,000 Salary For Managing Director: New Details In Rajesh Exports Probe
content: Despite the company reporting consolidated revenue of approximately Rs. 7.7 Lakh crore, the Chief Financial Officer has not received any salary since 2020. The Managing Director was paid only about Rs.17,000 a month, sources said.

title: Musk 'Poorer' By $363 Billion After SpaceX Wipeout, Loses Trillionaire Tag
content: According to the Bloomberg Billionaires Index, Musk's net worth has dropped to around $957 billion after reaching a record $1.32 trillion on June 16.

title: 10 Suspected Bangladeshi Nationals Detained In Guwahati: Sources
content: According to police sources, the individuals came under scrutiny during a routine checking drive. When questioned, they reportedly failed to provide satisfactory proof of identity or legal entry into India.

title: Judge Bars Trump From Implementing Proof Of Citizenship Requirement To Vote
content: The ruling by U.S. District Court Judge Denise Casper in Boston effectively converts a preliminary injunction she issued a year ago, in which she temporarily blocked many of Trump's efforts to overhaul elections, into a permanent ban.

title: After Lucknow Tragedy, Delhi Orders Safety Inspection Of 923 Coaching Centres
content: The building, which caught on fire in Lucknow, housed an animation centre, among other establishments, and most of the victims who lost their lives were students.

title: From Ranga-Billa to Nithari: Why some crimes simply refuse to fade from memory
content: While not jumping with joy seeing every dog that comes her way (to the bewilderment of the dog owner and the dog), Haimanti fantasizes about fantasy books or classics to read and re-read. That could be the gist of it all, except for the aroma of biryani that beckons; or that idea that's stuck in the head and refuses to go till it's penned down. Read More

title: Govt notifies interim list of 318 permissible development works under new rural employment framework
content: the framework under the Viksit Bharat - Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission (Gramin) Act 2025 from July 1 identifies four thematic focus areas . the interim list of 318 permissible works has been drawn based on these themes .

title: Airlines turn to AI to save fuel; IndiGo to start trials for thriftier take-offs from today
content: Indian airlines turn to artificial intelligence (AI) to save fuel . the airline aims to save 60-65 kilo per take off using this AI solution . it will start trials for thriftier take-offs on its Airbus fleet .

title: 3 pilgrims among 5 killed in J&K road accidents
content:
Representative image

JAMMU: Five persons, including three pilgrims, died and six others were injured on Wednesday in two separate road accidents in Jammu & Kashmir’s Doda and Reasi districts. Police said, three members of a family from Nagrota were killed while they were returning from the Machail Mata Yatra. The accident took place at 6.30pm in the Malhori area of Assar tehsil in Doda district. Two others sustained injuries. They were shifted to Doda govt medical college for treatment. In another accident, two women in a car died on Wednesday in the Chiral Shajroo area of Reasi district at around 1.30pm. The four others injured include three children. Authorities initiated a probe to ascertain the cause of the accident.

End of Article

title: 25 hurt as minibus plunges into gorge in J&K’s Kathua
content:
Representative image

JAMMU: At least 25 people were injured after a minibus ferrying pilgrims to Shri Sukrala Mata Temple in Billawer, in J&K’s Kathua district, plunged into a gorge in Simbli area on Wednesday. Officials said four of the injured were in critical condition. The minibus was ferrying devotees from Goran and Sumb villages in Samba district to the shrine. En route, the driver allegedly lost control of the vehicle near Dingi Simbli on Dhar Road, causing it to roll into the gorge, officials said. Locals, civic administration, and police personnel launched a joint rescue operation and rushed the injured occupants — including the driver — to a nearby health facility. Those in serious condition were later shifted to Kathua GMCH, officials said. Police registered a case and launched a probe to establish the cause of the accident.

End of Article

title: 30 India-bound ships crossed Strait of Hormuz, another 26 wait for their turn
content: 30 ships have crossed the strait of Hormuz and 26 wait to cross the critical sea route . 19 transits happened between march 1 and June 17 following the signing of the Iran-US MoU 11 ships . a maximum of five Marshall Islands-flagged ships are among the 26 vessels of Indian interest .

Renowned for his incisive coverage of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, accreditation reforms, and skilling initiatives, he has also reported on student politics, urban policy, and social movements. His political reportage—both reflective and news-driven—adds depth to his writing, bridging policy with public impact. title: Passport just a travel document, not citizenship proof: MEA
content: the passport attests the nationality of Indians when they are abroad but it isn't a document of citizenship, officials clarified in response to a query . this set off a raging discussion on X, with users asking what then counted as a conclusive proof of citizenship .
